  "reasoning": "Following the ordered skill graph strictly: Skill 1 is the strongest starting point: the terrain is clearly hilly with exposed rocky, pale soil and scrubby vegetation, which fits a karst landscape in the Western Balkans. From Skill 1 and Skill 2, the linked cues most consistent through the graph are Skill 5, Skill 6, Skill 10, and Skill 12: the light-colored rough pavement is common in former Yugoslav rural areas; the isolated simple rectangular white house with a red-tiled roof is very typical of rural Croatia/Bosnia and Herzegovina/Montenegro; the lack of lane markings and the road narrowness suggest southern Europe; and the road curvature with surrounding foothill terrain supports a mountainous Adriatic hinterland setting. Skills 3, 4, 7, 9, and 11 are less consistent with the full scene: Bulgaria is weaker than the western Adriatic karst fit, Germany/Czechia do not match the overall vegetation, road width, and house style, southern India/Sri Lanka is incompatible with the terrain and road context, and Scandinavian wooden-house cues are not present. Combining the high-priority terrain signal with the linked former Yugoslav architectural and road-surface cues gives the best consistent match as Montenegro. Within Montenegro, the dry limestone hills and rural setting fit especially well around Cetinje in the inland karst area.",

    "Very narrow rural paved road without center lines or shoulder",

    "Sparse scrub and mixed deciduous greenery in a dry sunny environment",

  ]

nodes.skill_text: The terrain is hilly with rocky soil visible in some places, which fits with the karst landscapes found in the Western Balkans.
The road is unpaved and appears to be a rural residential street, common in Eastern European countries.
The architecture style, with the house coloring and the tiled roof, is typical for rural Bulgarian towns.
The white road delineators with a single black rectangle are characteristic of those found in Germany and the Czech Republic, specifically in rural areas.
The road has a rough, light-colored surface, common in rural areas of southern Europe, especially the former Yugoslav countries.
The lone house has simple rectangular architecture with a red-tiled roof, a design frequently seen in rural Croatia, Bosnia and Herzegovina, and Montenegro.
The paved but narrow road without a shoulder is common in rural parts of Germany.
The architecture of the houses in the distance, particularly the red-tiled roofs and low-rise, wide structures, is very common in Southern or Eastern Europe.
The visible house has a tiled roof which is typical for villages in southern India and coastal Sri Lanka, but especially common in rural Tamil Nadu.
Absence of lane markings and the narrowness of the road suggest a rural location in southern Europe.
The architecture features simple, wooden houses painted white, which is common in Scandinavian countries, particularly in rural Sweden and Norway.
The curve and landscape of the road suggest a mountainous area, likely in the foothills rather than high altitude, which is common in southern Europe.
